evals.prompt: I'm building a signup form in React for our app. I need fields for full name, work email, a role dropdown (member / admin / billing), and a checkbox to accept terms. We use @uniform-ts/core already. Can you wire it up so it validates and gives me typed values on submit?
evals.expected_output: A React component that defines a single Zod V4 schema (z.object with z.string name, z.email, z.enum role, z.boolean terms), wraps it with createForm, and renders <AutoForm form={...} onSubmit={...}>. Validation messages live in the schema; onSubmit receives typed values. No manual register() calls or hand-wired inputs.
expectations: Defines one Zod schema as the source of truth using z.object
Imports z from 'zod/v4' and createForm/AutoForm from '@uniform-ts/core'
Uses createForm(schema) to wrap the schema
Renders <AutoForm> with form and onSubmit props
Uses z.enum for the role dropdown rather than hand-building a select
Validation messages are placed in the schema (e.g. .min(1, '...') or z.email('...'))
Does not call react-hook-form register() or build raw <input> elements manually

evals.prompt: Using UniForm, I have an order form where the user picks an account type (personal vs business). When they pick business, I want to reveal VAT number and company name fields, and hide them again for personal. There's also a repeating list of line items (description + quantity), minimum 1, maximum 5 rows. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: A schema with accountType enum, optional business fields, and a z.array(z.object(...)).min(1).max(5) for line items. Conditional fields use setCondition (or inline condition in the fields prop) so hidden fields are unregistered. The array renders as repeating rows with min/max read from the schema.
expectations: Models line items as z.array(z.object(...)) with .min(1) and .max(5) on the array
Uses setCondition or the inline condition field-override to show/hide business fields
Conditional fields depend on the accountType value via a predicate over form values
Explains or relies on UniForm hiding/unregistering fields rather than conditionally rendering JSX manually
Does not manually enforce the 1-5 row limit in UI code instead of the schema

evals.prompt: We want all our @uniform-ts/core forms to use our design system text input and toggle, plus a custom star-rating widget for a 1-5 score field. I don't want to repeat the component config on every form. What's the idiomatic way to do this?
evals.expected_output: Use createAutoForm once to bake in design-system defaults (components for string/boolean, etc.), returning a reusable AutoForm component. Register a custom 'rating' component typed with FieldProps<number> and point the score field at it via fields={{ score: { component: 'rating' } }}.
expectations: Uses createAutoForm to bake design-system defaults once instead of repeating per form
Maps string/boolean Zod types to the design-system components via the components registry
Defines a custom rating component typed with FieldProps<number>
Points the score field at the custom component via the fields prop (component key)
Distinguishes createAutoForm (design-system defaults) from createForm (per-schema definition)

evals.prompt: We're building a fairly large multi-step onboarding form in React with @uniform-ts/core: personal details, an employment section that only appears for 'employed' status, and a repeating list of references (1-3). I want this done cleanly with a clear separation between the data model and the UI, and I'd like the final result reviewed for any non-idiomatic UniForm usage before we ship. How should we approach this?
evals.expected_output: A response that decomposes the work across the skill's specialized agents: the Schema Architect designs the Zod schema (status enum, optional/conditional employment fields or discriminated union, references array with .min(1).max(3)); the Form Builder renders it (createForm/AutoForm, setCondition for employment, fields/sections, schema-driven array bounds); and the Form Reviewer audits the result for hallucinated APIs and schema-vs-fields violations. The data model stays separate from presentation throughout.
expectations: Recognizes the work should be split across the bundled specialized agents rather than done in one undifferentiated pass
Assigns the Zod schema design (status enum, conditional employment fields, references array with .min(1)/.max(3)) to the Schema Architect role
Assigns the render layer (createForm/AutoForm, setCondition, fields/sections, schema-driven array bounds) to the Form Builder role
Assigns a read-only idiomatic audit (hallucinated APIs, schema-vs-fields boundary) to the Form Reviewer role
Keeps the data model (schema + validation) separate from presentation (fields/components) per the skill's core boundary
Does not invent UniForm APIs (no useForm/Form/Field/register, no createForm second-arg options, no defineRegistry/overrides)
